xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:AddressOfRegisteredOfficeOfCom: B1/B2, 7th Floor, 701, Marathon Innova, Ganpatrao Kadam Marg, Lower Parel, Mumbai – 400013
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:AddressOfCorporateOfficeOfComp: B1/B2, 7th Floor, 701, Marathon Innova, Ganpatrao Kadam Marg, Lower Parel, Mumbai – 400013
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:NameOfContactPerson: Mr. Pratik Pota, Managing Director & Chief Executive Officer
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:NotesGeneralDisclosureExplanat:
1. Question 23 - a) The Company takes all active measures to include its subsidiaries in its Business responsibility Initiatives b) Forbes Lux FZE, Dubai is a wholly-owned subsidiary of Euro Forbes Limited, Dubai (Wholly Owned Subsidiary of Eureka Forbes Limited).
2. Question 24 - The Turnover and Net worth details as mentioned are in Lakhs. i.e. The Company's turnover is Rs. 2,43,606.24 Lakhs and Rs. 4,38,014.34 Lakhs.
3.Question 26 - The Customer complaints as mentioned pertains to Product, Services and Advertising related matters.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DescriptionOfBusinessActivity: Electrical equipment, General Purpose and Special purpose Machinery & equipment, Transport equipment
Repair & installation of machinery & equipment, motor vehicles
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:ProductOrServiceSoldByTheEntit: Manufacture of filtering and purifying machinery or apparatus for liquids and gases
Repair and servicing of household appliances
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:ABriefOnTypesOfCustomersExplan: The Company is committed to serving a diverse customer base across multiple sectors, including B2C, educational institutions, retail stores, households, B2B clients, banks, IT firms, hospitals, SMEs, and hotels. Its market presence also spans government bodies such as the railways, armed forces, and various departments through platforms like the Government e-Marketplace (GeM). To ensure extensive market reach, the Company utilises its own website, leading e-commerce platforms, modern retail chains, and general trade outlets.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:WebLinkForGrievanceRedressPoli: Link to grievance redressal mechanism is www. eurekaforbes.com/ connectwith-us\
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsForGrievanceRedressalMe: Not Applicable as the Company doesn’t have any investors other than Equity Shareholder
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:WebLinkForGrievanceRedressPoli: Link to grievance redressal mechanism is www.eurekaforbes.com/connectwith-us
Link to grievance redressal mechanism is www.eurekaforbes.com/connectwith-us
Call Centre, WhatsApp, Email, IVR, Website, Customer App www.eurekaforbes.com/connectwith-us
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:RemarksStakeHolderGroupFromWho: Customer concerns are addressed promptly and effectively, ensuring a high level of satisfaction with complaint resolution.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:WebLinkForGrievanceRedressPoli: We engage & interact with suppliers regularly and through these interactions with suppliers on regular basis , we seek their feedback & resolve & addresses grievances if any
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:RationaleForIdentifyingTheRisk: Inability to drive category growth and market share.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:InCaseOfRiskApproachToAdaptOrM: 1. Significant investments are made in R&D and marketing capabilities. 2. Our offerings encompass multiple price points – from entry level to premium. The diverse price points are available for both products and services accross categories. 3. We have an innovation roadmap to ensure a steady pipeline of unique innovations. 4. Our omnichannel approach, including strong go-to-market presence and access to consumer homes provides extensive market reach.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:RationaleForIdentifyingTheRisk: Risk associated with poor customer experience potential impact on our reputation.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:InCaseOfRiskApproachToAdaptOrM: 1. Our extensive service network is being fortified with infrastructure upgrades and expansion. 2. Continuous onboarding of service technicians and Partners with training helps in improving the NPS score . 3. Significant investments in digital capabilities empower customers by giving them greater control over their experience. Initiatives like “Customer Day” and extensive engagement with business partners foster a strong customer-centric mindset across the organisation.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:RationaleForIdentifyingTheRisk: Risk arising from unexpected disruptions in the supply chain due to geopolitical or other external factors.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:InCaseOfRiskApproachToAdaptOrM: 1. Our supply network is derisked through a detailed vendor development roadmap. 2. Robust strategic partnerships are in place and are continuously enhanced. 3. Inhouse and Domestic manufacturing partnerships in key categories helps in maintaining healthy supply of products.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:RationaleForIdentifyingTheRisk: Risk associated with cybersecurity, including cyberattacks, ransomware, data breaches, inadequate disaster recovery, and non-compliance with evolving data privacy laws such as DPDP Rules.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:InCaseOfRiskApproachToAdaptOrM: 1. Detailed disaster recovery process is in place, ensuring business continuity. 2. We have implemented several data security measures in areas like customer data, call masking, etc. to safeguard sensitive information. 3. Cybersecurity awareness trainings have been rolled out across the organisation. 4. We maintain in house capabilities and access to external cybersecurity experts.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:RationaleForIdentifyingTheRisk: Risk associated with people and cultural factors that may hinder our transformation initiatives.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:InCaseOfRiskApproachToAdaptOrM: 1. We have conducted strategy alignment sessions and cascaded them across all employees. The first-ever ESOP programme covering all managers was rolled out. 2. Sustained employee engagement and recognition programmes across levels are in place to drive the transformation agenda. 3. We have built new people capabilities and scaled up in areas like R&D, Technology, D2C, and Sales organisation. 4. ‘Voice of EFL’ engagement survey with a high participation rate helped reinforce employee centricity while identifying collaboration gaps
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:RationaleForIdentifyingTheRisk: Risk associated with inability to enhance product quality, meet evolving customer needs, adapt to rapid technological shifts and market trends, and comply with environmental regulations.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:InCaseOfRiskApproachToAdaptOrM: 1. Design improvements, including new flow restrictors and flushing mechanisms, are underway, along with regular review of performance metrics. 2. Our R&D and marketing pipelines are aligned with a multi-year portfolio plan, while new technologies and product variants have been launched. 3. Both factories are EMS certified with EPR compliance in place. Annual filings and waste management ensure regulatory adherence. CO2 emissions are monitored, with a decarbonisation roadmap targeting measurable reductions by FY26.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:NameOfTheNationalAndInternatio: 1. GC Mark Certificate (Bengaluru & Dehradun) 2. IGBC Membership Certificate (Dehradun) 3. ISO 14001: 2015 (Environment Management System) (Bengaluru & Dehradun) 4. ISO 9001: 2015 (Quality Management System) (Bengaluru & Dehradun) 5. BIS Certification as per IS 14724 : 1999 (Dehradun) 6. IS16240 certifications (Bengaluru & Dehradun) 7. IS14724 Certifications (Dehradun) 8. IS0 45001:2018 (Occupational Health and Safety (OH&S)Management Systems) (Dehradun & Bengaluru)
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:SpecificCommitmentsGoalsAndTar:
The Company is committed to delivering long-term sustainable value to its stakeholders. While the comprehensive framework for setting long-term targets is currently under development, we have proactively initiated early-stage measures to promote Environmental, Social, and Governance (ESG) principles.
For the fiscal year 2025-26, the Company has set the following specific goals with defined timelines: 1. Reduction in Groundwater Withdrawal: The manufacturing plants aim to achieve an additional 5% reduction in groundwater withdrawal compared to the previous year.
2. Renewable Energy Initiatives: Installation of rooftop solar plants with capacities of 245 kWp and 165 kWp at the manufacturing facilities (Dehradun & Bengaluru) is planned within FY 2025- 26. This initiative supports our renewable energy goals and helps reduce dependence on conventional power sources.
3. Energy Efficient Equipment & Infrastructure: Installation of VFD Air Compressors and high efficiency assembly line drives. These targeted actions underscore our commitment to sustainable growth and ESG integration as part of our broader corporate strategy.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:PerformanceOfTheEntityAgainstT:
Ground Water Withdrawal: In FY 2024-25, both our plants successfully achieved a 10% reduction in groundwater withdrawal compared to the previous financial year. This accomplishment was driven by initiatives including the use of RO reject water for cleaning & flushing, as well as the implementation of digital water meters for close monitoring of daily water consumption. These measures have enabled more efficient water management and contributed significantly to our sustainability goals.
ISO 45001:2018 Certification: In FY 2024-25, both our plants successfully obtained the OHS & MS certification following comprehensive audits conducted by an external agency. This achievement was realised alongside significant upgrades to the safety protocols within our premises, reinforcing our commitment to a safe and compliant work environment.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:StatementByDirectorResponsible:
The Company has always believed that sustainability and business growth go hand-in-hand. Our consumer-centric approach has helped us stay a leader in the home appliances market by quickly adapting to changing consumer needs. We are committed to delivering high product standards and exceptional customer service while embedding sustainable practices into every part of our business. This includes optimising energy use, adopting eco-friendly packaging, prioritising local sourcing, conserving resources, and reducing waste across our operations. Responsible sourcing throughout our supply chain is also a key focus, helping us minimise our environmental footprint and support a sustainable future.
In FY 2024–25, we made significant strides toward our environmental goals, with our manufacturing facilities in Dehradun and Bengaluru achieving a 10% and 8% reduction in groundwater withdrawal respectively, through the implementation of water reuse systems and digital monitoring technologies. Looking ahead to FY 2025–26, we aim to further reduce groundwater use by 5% and install rooftop solar plants with capacities of 246.5 kWp and 165 kWp. These steps reflect our commitment to tackling ESG challenges like climate resilience and energy transition. While developing a comprehensive long-term ESG roadmap, we remain focussed on continuous improvement, transparent reporting, and aligning with global standards to create lasting value for all stakeholders.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfTheHighestAuthorityRe: Mr. Pratik Pota DIN: 00751178 Designation: Managing Director & CEO
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfSpecifiedCommitteeOfT: The Managing Director & CEO, along with senior management, continuously oversee the Company’s social, environmental, governance, and economic responsibilities

xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:NameOfTheAgencyIfTheEntityCarr: The Board undertakes quarterly reviews and internal audits of all approved policies. To further enhance governance and risk management, an external agency has been engaged to independently assess the effectiveness of policies,operational processes, and compliance mechanisms.

The Board undertakes quarterly reviews and internal audits of all approved policies. To further enhance governance and risk management, an external agency has been engaged to independently assess the effectiveness of policies,operational processes, and compliance mechanisms.

xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:TopicsOrPrinciplesCoveredUnder: 1. Prevention of Sexual Harassment 2. Code of Coduct 3. Prohibition of Insider Trading Training
1. Prevention of Sexual Harassment 2. Code of Coduct 3. Prohibition of Insider Trading Training
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:AntiCorruptionOrAntiBriberyPol:
The Company ensures that all policies and practices align with the National Voluntary Guidelines (NVGs), with their implementation monitored by relevant Board Committees and Functional Heads. To foster awareness and accessibility, all HR policies are hosted on the Company’s employee portal, enabling seamless communication and transparency.Statutory policies are publicly available on the Company’s website (www.eurekaforbes.com).
The Company has established a formal Code of Conduct and Code of Ethics, supported by a robust Whistle Blower Policy applicable to both internal and external stakeholders. A comprehensive e-learning module on these policies is mandatory for all employees and forms a part of the induction for new joiners. Additionally, the course must be completed annually by every employee to reinforce a culture of ethics, transparency, and accountability. For workers, quarterly refresher sessions are conducted by HR teams to maintain consistent awareness and understanding of these critical policies.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:NotesPrinciple1ExplanatoryText:
Question 1: Employees otherthan BoD and KMPs and workers the following trainings and the percentage of coverage: 1. Prevention of Sexual Harassment - 99% 2. Code of Conduct - 100% 3. Prevention of Insider Trading - 100%
Question 2: Refer to the Company’s website for all disclosures made under Regulation 30 of SEBI (Listing Obligations and Disclosure Obligations) Regulations, 2015 at www.eurekaforbes.com/investor-relations/corporate-announcements
Question 3: Refer to the Company’s website for all disclosures made under Regulation 30 of SEBI (Listing Obligations and Disclosure Obligations) Regulations, 2015 at www.eurekaforbes.com/investor-relations/corporate-announcements.
Question 9: The "Number of dealers distributors to whom sales are made" reported in Financial Year 2023-24 includes Institutional Customers
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfImprovementsInEnviron: Heavy Metal Remover Technology: Integrated Heavy Metal Remover Cartridge into UV water purification systems to enhance safety and improved water quality. Water Saver Technology: Implemented across all RO water purifier models, this technology ensures a minimum 40% water recovery rate, meeting BIS standards and advancing our commitment to sustainability.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DescribeTheProcessesInPlaceToS:
The Company has collaborated with India’s leading waste management and disposal companies to address plastic waste responsibly. The Company aims to minimise the environmental impact of plastic waste and contribute to a circular economy by promoting the recycling and reuse of plastics used in packaging.
By partnering with authorised waste management companies, the Company demonstrates its dedication to environmental stewardship and taking concrete steps towards a greener future. The company has obtained EPR registration for Plastic Waste management.

The Company has partnered with leading electronic asset management and disposal companies for its e-waste recycling initiative. Through this collaboration, Eureka Forbes ensures environmentally safe management of electronics at their end-of-life phase, contributing to environmental protection and responsible waste management. The company has obtained EPR registration for E- Waste management.

The Company has taken significant steps to address environmental concerns at its factories in Dehradun and Bommassandra, Bengaluru. The Company has installed Effluent Treatment Plants and Sewage Treatment Plants to effectively remove contaminants from wastewater and sewage. The treated water is collected in storage tanks and reused for gardening and other applications, promoting sustainable water management practices. Additionally, the Company has identified reliable and renowned partners, who are India’s leading waste management and disposal companies, to efficiently handle the waste generated during their operations. This proactive approach highlights the Company’s commitment to responsible waste management and environmental stewardship.
The Company has executed an agreement with the government authorised vendor for collection and recycling of bio medical waste at both factory locations.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:NotesPrinciple2ExplanatoryText:
1. Question 2a: Yes, the Company recommends and ensures that suppliers adhere to social and ethical standards throughout the supply chain. The company requires declarations from suppliers and restricts hazardous materials in accordance with RoHS regulations. For example, it verifies the absence of restricted substances in PCBs and reviews material composition, obtaining Material Test Certificates for parts such as stainless steel tanks whenever necessary. The Company also recommends and prioritises the adoption of sustainable practices among supply chain partners by promoting the use of reusable PP boxes for plastic parts. Additionally, it is now encouraging the use of honeycomb materials for internal packaging and is actively progressing in this direction.
2. Question 2b: The sustaiable sourcing is ~ 20%
3.Question 4: EPR is applicable to the Company.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:WebLinkOfEqualOppertunityPolic: The Company is committed to fostering a fair, inclusive, and discrimination-free workplace for all employees, applicants, and workers. Equal opportunities are provided to every individual, irrespective of race, gender, religion, disability, age, sexual orientation, gender identity, or any other characteristic. The Company strictly prohibits discrimination of any kind, as outlined in the Employee Code of Conduct. The principle of equal opportunity is also firmly embedded in the Company’s Code of Ethics and Conduct Policy.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfMechanismAvailableToR:
EUREKA FORBES LIMITED (EFL) is an equal employment opportunity company and is committed to creating a healthy working environment. The policies of the organisation strictly prohibits discrimination against any employee or applicant for employment on the grounds of the individual’s race, colour, religion, gender, sexual orientation, national origin, age, disability or any other characteristic protected by law. EFL ensures that all employment decisions, involving recruitment, hiring, promotion, training, compensation, benefits, transfer, discipline, and discharge, are all free of unlawful discrimination.
To reinforce its commitment to create a healthy work environment that enables employees to work without fear of prejudice, bias or harassment, the company has put in place a well defined grievance redressal mechanism. Commitees are setup at regional levels and manufacturing units, Employee Assitance Registers (EAR) are maintained in Units, and regular awareness sessions are held to ensure that any complaints or concerns of employees are heard and amicably resolved. These mechanisms are designed to receive and facilitate the resolution of concerns raised by employees and to address complaints, disputes, or grievances brought forward by external stakeholders. It serves as an important channel for individuals to seek redress and ensures that their concerns are handled in a fair and timely manner. By involving various functions and establishing robust mechanisms, EFL strives to create a work environment that respects and safeguards human rights. The Company is committed to addressing any human rights issues that may arise and continuously improving practices to uphold the well-being and dignity of all individuals impacted by our operations.

EUREKA FORBES LIMITED (EFL) is an equal employment opportunity company and is committed to creating a healthy working environment. The policies of the organisation strictly prohibits discrimination against any employee or applicant for employment on the grounds of the individual’s race, colour, religion, gender, sexual orientation, national origin, age, disability or any other characteristic protected by law. EFL ensures that all employment decisions, involving recruitment, hiring, promotion, training, compensation, benefits, transfer, discipline, and discharge, are all free of unlawful discrimination.
To reinforce its commitment to create a healthy work environment that enables employees to work without fear of prejudice, bias or harassment, the company has put in place a well defined grievance redressal mechanism. Commitees are setup at regional levels and manufacturing units, Employee Assitance Registers (EAR) are maintained in Units, and regular awareness sessions are held to ensure that any complaints or concerns of employees are heard and amicably resolved. These mechanisms are designed to receive and facilitate the resolution of concerns raised by employees and to address complaints, disputes, or grievances brought forward by external stakeholders. It serves as an important channel for individuals to seek redress and ensures that their concerns are handled in a fair and timely manner. By involving various functions and establishing robust mechanisms, EFL strives to create a work environment that respects and safeguards human rights. The Company is committed to addressing any human rights issues that may arise and continuously improving practices to uphold the well-being and dignity of all individuals impacted by our operations.

EUREKA FORBES LIMITED (EFL) is an equal employment opportunity company and is committed to creating a healthy working environment. The policies of the organisation strictly prohibits discrimination against any employee or applicant for employment on the grounds of the individual’s race, colour, religion, gender, sexual orientation, national origin, age, disability or any other characteristic protected by law. EFL ensures that all employment decisions, involving recruitment, hiring, promotion, training, compensation, benefits, transfer, discipline, and discharge, are all free of unlawful discrimination.
To reinforce its commitment to create a healthy work environment that enables employees to work without fear of prejudice, bias or harassment, the company has put in place a well defined grievance redressal mechanism. Commitees are setup at regional levels and manufacturing units, Employee Assitance Registers (EAR) are maintained in Units, and regular awareness sessions are held to ensure that any complaints or concerns of employees are heard and amicably resolved. These mechanisms are designed to receive and facilitate the resolution of concerns raised by employees and to address complaints, disputes, or grievances brought forward by external stakeholders. It serves as an important channel for individuals to seek redress and ensures that their concerns are handled in a fair and timely manner. By involving various functions and establishing robust mechanisms, EFL strives to create a work environment that respects and safeguards human rights. The Company is committed to addressing any human rights issues that may arise and continuously improving practices to uphold the well-being and dignity of all individuals impacted by our operations.

EUREKA FORBES LIMITED (EFL) is an equal employment opportunity company and is committed to creating a healthy working environment. The policies of the organisation strictly prohibits discrimination against any employee or applicant for employment on the grounds of the individual’s race, colour, religion, gender, sexual orientation, national origin, age, disability or any other characteristic protected by law. EFL ensures that all employment decisions, involving recruitment, hiring, promotion, training, compensation, benefits, transfer, discipline, and discharge, are all free of unlawful discrimination.
To reinforce its commitment to create a healthy work environment that enables employees to work without fear of prejudice, bias or harassment, the company has put in place a well defined grievance redressal mechanism. Commitees are setup at regional levels and manufacturing units, Employee Assitance Registers (EAR) are maintained in Units, and regular awareness sessions are held to ensure that any complaints or concerns of employees are heard and amicably resolved. These mechanisms are designed to receive and facilitate the resolution of concerns raised by employees and to address complaints, disputes, or grievances brought forward by external stakeholders. It serves as an important channel for individuals to seek redress and ensures that their concerns are handled in a fair and timely manner. By involving various functions and establishing robust mechanisms, EFL strives to create a work environment that respects and safeguards human rights. The Company is committed to addressing any human rights issues that may arise and continuously improving practices to uphold the well-being and dignity of all individuals impacted by our operations.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfOccupationalHealthAnd: Yes, the Company has implemented the ISO 45001:2018 Occupational Health and Safety Management System. This system provides a structured framework to manage OH&S risks and opportunities, aiming to prevent work-related injuries and ill health, and to ensure a safe and healthy workplace by eliminating hazards and reducing risks through effective preventive and protective measures. The scope of ISO 45001:2018 certification covers Eureka’s sites, including both manufacturing locations.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DesclosureOfTheProcessesUsedTo: The Company conducts monthly evaluations for routine job tasks, which includes: 1. Gemba Walk assessment. 2. Internal and External Audit reviews. 3. Near Miss Reporting for the Employees & Visitors. 4. Quarterly Work Zone Monitoring for illumination and noise level. For non-routine jobs, the Company implements a Work Permit System (WPS) to ensure proper authorisation and safety measures..
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DescribeTheMeasuresTakenByTheE: The Company places strong emphasis on the safety and well-being of employees and workers by adopting preventive measures to ensure a safe work environment. This includes maintaining an in-house trained Emergency Response Team and conducting safety inductions for all new joiners. Regular health and safety training sessions are organised to equip employees with the knowledge to manage potential risks effectively.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfAnyCorrectiveActionTa: During the reporting year, all identified safety-related observations and unsafe conditions were resolved by the factory management team. No major risks or concerns were identified during the health and safety assessments. Audit findings were systematically converted into risk mitigation plans, and specific corrective actions were implemented to eliminate potential hazards. The Company has established robust systems to identify, monitor, and address unsafe conditions across operational sites. Dedicated safety officers conduct regular inspections and maintain documentation, with monthly management reviews to track the status of corrective actions. At office locations, audit observations are addressed through targeted action plans led by local Finance, HR, and Administration teams. All necessary actions have been taken, and any open points are regularly reviewed and closed in a timely manner.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:NotesPrinciple3ExplanatoryText:
1. Question 1c: Please note, the Company has only considered Staff welfare expenses and health insurance premium for the above.
2. Question 3 : The Company recognises the importance of complying with the Rights of Persons with Disabilities Act, 2016, and is taking proactive steps to create an inclusive and accessible work environment. While there are currently no permanent employees with disabilities, the Company is progressively implementing accessibility features such as ramps, signage, and restrooms across its premises. Accessibility is viewed as a key element of social responsibility, and continuous efforts are being made to foster a barrier-free and supportive workplace for all.
3. Question 7: Note: All employees and workers at EFL are free to form or join associations of their choice and engage in collective bargaining. The Company also expects its suppliers and vendors to uphold and respect these principles, ensuring freedom of association and collective bargaining throughout the value chain.
4. Question 10c: Yes, the Company has put in place a well-defined set of processes to address work-related hazards effectively. These processes empower workers to report any potential risks or unsafe conditions they encounter during their work tasks. The Company’s commitment to creating a safe work environment through effective hazard reporting and risk avoidance demonstrates their dedication to the well-being of their workers.
5. Question 10d: Yes, the employees of the Company have access to non-occupational medical and healthcare services and all the employees are covered under healthcare Insurance. In addition to this, we have established OHC (Occupational Health Canter) and also signed the agreement with the hospital at both the locations, on demand Ambulance and Doctor’s visits.
6. Question 14: 100%. Inspections and annual audits are carried out at factory locations by the Factory Inspector and external audit partners to review overall plant operations, including health and safety aspects.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DescribeTheProcessesForIdentif:
The Company places a strong emphasis on nurturing meaningful relationships with both internal and external stakeholders, recognising their impact on business decisions and operations. Trust built through these relationships reinforces the Company’s brand reputation and long-term value creation. A structured stakeholder mapping exercise has been undertaken to support strategic decision-making and ensure alignment with stakeholder expectations.
As referenced in Principle 4, Question 2, the Company has set up effective communication channels to maintain transparent and open dialogue with all key stakeholder groups, including: a. Employees b. Local Community c. Supplier/Vendor d. Shareholders/Investors e. Customers f. Regulatory Bodies g. Trade Partners This approach enables the Company to identify, prioritise, and respond to the needs and concerns of each group,fostering sustained, collaborative, and value-driven engagement.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:NotesPrinciple4ExplanatoryText: Question 2: Although we have indicated ‘No’ above, as these stakeholder groups are not entirely vulnerable or marginalised, the Company is consciously engaged in initiatives aimed at supporting and empowering vulnerable segments within these groups. For instance, India’s first call centre operated by individuals with special needs has been established in collaboration with NASEOH (National Society for Equal Opportunities for the handicapped) in Chembur, Mumbai.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfOtherChannelsOfCommun: 1. SMARTs (national and regional) 2. Monthly regional townhalls; Quarterly central townhall (led by ExCom) 3. Regular communication from Corp Com and HR on key updates 4. Engagement activities (sports, celebrations, etc.) – annual engagement calendar published
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:PurposeAndScopeOfEngagementInc: To support employee development, ensure well-being and maintain a safe workplace
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfOtherChannelsOfCommun: 1. Eco School project, impacting more than 4500 children and staff from 12 schools across Dehradun, Bengaluru, & Mumbai. 2. Built and refurbishment of more than 51 toilets for boys and girls along with set up of 18 water purifiers across the schools. 3. WASH (Water, Sanitation, and Hygiene) awareness trainings conducted to encourage critical hygiene practices and extend their influence to families and communities. 4. Each school handover ceremony had senior leaders from EFL personally visiting these schools and meeting school management committees to hand over the facilities.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:PurposeAndScopeOfEngagementInc: Local communities influence public perception and company reputation. NGOs act as facilitators in community engagement and advocacy with local authorities
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfOtherChannelsOfCommun: In-person meetings, vendor visits or visits to vendor’s facility/office, engagement at leadership level
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:PurposeAndScopeOfEngagementInc: Ensuring supply chain continuity, quality assurance, and longterm collaboration on future business needs
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfOtherChannelsOfCommun: Quarterly earnings Calls, Investors Meet, Annual General Meeting, Company Website, Press Releases and Other Statutory Disclosures.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:PurposeAndScopeOfEngagementInc: Shareholders provide financial backing and credibility. Transparent engagement is key to reinforcing trust and market confidence
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfOtherChannelsOfCommun: Employee Visits, CRM activities, NPS surveys, Website
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:PurposeAndScopeOfEngagementInc: To strengthen customer relationships and maintain predictable revenue growth
Fostering compliance, transparency, and accountability, thereby building long-term trust
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:StakeholderGroup: Trade Partners – FBPs (Franchise Business Partners), FDOs (Franchise Direct Operations) and SSDs (Sales & Service Distributors)
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfOtherChannelsOfCommun: ERP & DMS – Primary & Secondary Sales Order Processing & Data Archiving JOS (Joy of selling) Proprietary App – Lead management system; Manpower active count, demo trends & sales productivity management. Partner Circle Head – Company SPOC responsible for individual Partner Performance Management.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:PurposeAndScopeOfEngagementInc: Strategic collaboration to enhance market reach in non-CRC zones and (company owned & operated “Customer Response Centres”) or key cities/markets
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DescribeTheInternalMechanismsI: To maintain a safe and productive workplace, the Company has adopted a POSH policy and a whistle-blower policy.Detailed response on the same is shared in P5-Q8. The Company has zero tolerance for human rights violations. Human rights is one of the Company’s key focus areas. Any human rights violation, wherever reported, shall be investigated by the Management following the code of conduct policy of the Company.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:MechanismsToPreventAdverseCons:
The Company maintains a strict policy against discrimination and harassment, ensuring that all investigations are conducted confidentially to protect the complainant and witnesses from any form of disadvantage. In alignment with the POSH policy implemented across the organisation, appropriate measures are taken to safeguard individuals who report incidents, protecting them from victimisation or retaliation.
Recognising the importance of a safe and supportive work environment, the Company encourages employees to voice their concerns without fear of adverse consequences. The POSH training programme, available on the Learning Management System (LMS), is a mandatory annual compliance requirement for all employees. To effectively address both sexual and non-sexual harassment, Internal Complaints Committees (ICCs) have been constituted at both regional and corporate offices. These committees comprise a diverse panel of internal and external members with relevant expertise and operate under clearly defined procedures for addressing cases of sexual harassment.
Regular sensitisation and training sessions are conducted to promote awareness and responsiveness to these issues. These initiatives are coordinated by the Human Resources department, in collaboration with other relevant functions,to ensure consistent implementation across the Company.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:NotesPrinciple5ExplanatoryText:
Question 3b: FY 2024-25 are in Crores
Question 4: The Company follows a Code of Conduct, with the HR department responsible for managing any human rights concerns arising from business operations. Additionally, a whistleblower mechanism is in place, enabling employees (current or former), directors, business partners, distributors, customers, or vendors to report concerns confidentially.
Question 9: No. However, the Company strongly believes that suppliers and vendors are an integral part of its business and contribute to its growth and viability. Thus regular engagement activities are organised with suppliers and vendors
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfCoverageAndImplementa: Yes, Eureka has implemented a Zero Liquid Discharge (ZLD) system at both factory locations. Sewage Treatment Plants (STPs) and Effluent Treatment Plants (ETPs) have been installed, and the treated water is recycled and reused within the premises for activities such as gardening and flushing. A minor quantity of water is lost during the treatment and sludge drying process due to evaporation, while the remaining treated water is fully utilised for internal purposes. The Company adheres to all applicable guidelines and standards prescribed by the Central and State Pollution Control Boards (CPCB and SPCBs) for the operation and maintenance of STPs and ETPs.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfProjectRelatedToReduc: 1. The Company has transitioned from R-22 to R-32 refrigerants in air conditioning systems, leading to a reduction in both ozone depletion potential (ODP) and CO2 emissions. 2. In FY 2024-25, the manufacturing operations contributed to carbon emission reduction by replacing a petrol car with a hybrid (petrol + CNG) vehicle at the Bengaluru plant and by shifting from petrol scooters to electric scooters at the Dehradun plant.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfWasteManagementPracti: 1. The Company has established well-defined systems to manage operational waste efficiently. In line with the Extended Producer Responsibility (EPR) guidelines under the Plastic Waste Management Rules, 2016 (amended in 2022), the Company ensures the collection, processing, and recycling of an amount of plastic waste equivalent to what is used in its product packaging annually. Although hazardous waste generation is minimal compared to non-hazardous waste, robust mechanisms are in place for its safe collection, transportation, and disposal through authorised government vendors. These efforts reflect the Company’s commitment to responsible waste management and environmentally sustainable practices. 2. The Company is also compliant with the E-Waste Management System, as per the E-Waste (Management) Rules,2016 (amended in 2022).
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:LocationOfOperationsOrOffices: Eureka Forbes Limited, Lal Tappar Industrial Area, Majri Grant, Dehradun Haridwar Highway -Dehradun, Uttarakhand - 248140
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:TypeOfOperations: Assembling Unit of Water Filter cum Purifier & Water Cooler Cum Purifier
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:NotesPrinciple6ExplanatoryText:
Please note the Revenue from Operation is in Millons.
Question 1, 3, 4, 6, 7, and 9: The above details pertains to manufacturing plants located at Dehradun and Bengaluru.
Question 2: The Company currently has no sites or facilities classified as Designated Consumers (DCs) under the Government of India’s Perform, Achieve and Trade (PAT) Scheme.
Question 6: Yes, the Company has obtained Environmental Clearance from the Ministry of Environment, Forest, and Climate Change for Lal Tappar area
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:NameOfTheTradeAndIndustryChamb: International Market Assessment India Private Ltd. (IMA)
Associated Chambers of Commerce and Industry of India (ASSOCHAM)
Federation of Indian Chambers of Commerce and Industry (FICCI)
Bombay Chambers of Commerce and Industry
IMC Chamber of Commerce and Industry
The Advertising Standards Council of India
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DescribeTheMechanismsToReceive: The Company actively engages in community programmes aimed at fostering strong relationships. Through regular interactions and attentive listening, the Company works to understand and respond to community concerns. Timely and effective actions are prioritised, reflecting the Company’s commitment to supporting community wellbeing.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:NotesPrinciple8ExplanatoryText:
Question 4:
For FY 2023-24: *Sourcing from Factory - Directly sourced from MSME/Small producers **Sourcing from within 250 Km of Factory - Sourced directly from within the districts and neigbouring districts
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DescribeTheMechanismsInPlaceTo: The Company has a robust system in place to manage customer complaints and feedback effectively. Customers can connect through various channels, including the Call Centre, WhatsApp, Email, IVR, and the official website. Further contact details are available at www.eurekaforbes.com/contactus. An Escalation Management Team (EMT) operates from both the head office and key strategic locations. A structured escalation hierarchy is in place, with dedicated Service Heads in each region supported by a team of experienced service leaders to ensure timely and satisfactory resolution of customer issues.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:RemarkForConsumerComplaints: Consumer complaints received via the Advertising Standards Council of India have been successfully resolved.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:NotesPrinciple9ExplanatoryText:
Question 3: Others consists of: " The Complaints pertain to product and service-related matter."
Question 5: The Company complies with all applicable standards and regulations related to cybersecurity and data privacy. An Information Security Policy is in place, applicable to EFL Group employees, external and third-party personnel (including vendors, consultants, interns, and contractors), as well as clients or customers visiting EFL Group offices who have access to company information or information processing facilities. This policy is reviewed annually to ensure continued relevance and effectiveness. Additionally, the Company has implemented a Privacy Policy to safeguard customer data. For more details, refer to Privacy Policy.
